finding: A cryptographic verifier report cannot contribute to deployment acceptance unless its reported SHA-256 subject digest equals the exact cloudflare-deployment-proof.json bytes being evaluated.

behavior: downgrades verified reports with absent, malformed, or mismatched subject digests to not_verified
preserves separation between cryptographic provenance and deployment-claim truth

verification.method: isolated Node test using byte-identical proposed source and test content before commit
covered: local in-toto digest mismatch remains independently visible

privacy_boundary: No credentials, private chat, health information, personal identifiers, or secret values were read or committed.
limits: This does not configure Cloudflare credentials or produce a deployment.
A matching digest does not prove certificate-chain validity, trusted time, workflow authorization, served availability, or scientific truth.
Repository CI execution for the committed SHAs was not yet observed at record creation.
falsification_route: Supply a normalized verifier result marked verified whose subject_digest differs from the SHA-256 of proofText; acceptance input must mark signatureVerification not_verified and signatureSubjectVerification mismatch.
